message:
fix: Unicode-safe string truncation, JSONL byte tracking, velocity normalization, and misc correctness

Unicode-safe truncation (4 locations across mining.rs, mail.rs):
- All string truncation for display (titles, subject lines, sender names,
  body previews) now uses .chars().count() and .chars().take(n) instead
  of byte-based [..n] slicing, preventing panics on multi-byte UTF-8.

JSONL parser rewrite (rch.rs):
- Replace String::from_utf8_lossy + .lines() with manual byte-level line
  scanner, fixing bytes_read tracking accuracy (previously off-by-one per
  line and wrong for non-UTF8 content).

Pattern recognizer arg handling (autogen.rs):
- Use Vec<String> as HashMap key instead of joining args into a
  space-delimited string, preserving args that contain spaces.

Rate limit velocity normalization (rate_limit.rs):
- Divide usage-percent change by elapsed minutes between samples (was
  raw difference ignoring time delta). Filter out zero-duration windows.
- Replace cast_unsigned() with u64::try_from().unwrap_or(0) for portability.

Health factor parsing (lib.rs):
- Extract name and details from parsed details_json instead of raw JSON
  string or simple factor_id transform.

Progress bar overflow guard (machines.rs):
- Clamp filled bar width to widget width via .min(width), preventing
  panic if pct > 100.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
